---
title: "Agent Communication in ADK"
description: "How ADK agents send and receive messages"
---

# Agent Communication in ADK

## Messages in ADK

ADK uses `google.genai.types.Content` for messages — the same format as the Gemini API:

```python
from google.genai import types

# User message
user_msg = types.Content(
    role="user",
    parts=[types.Part(text="What is machine learning?")]
)

# Agent response (generated by the model)
agent_msg = types.Content(
    role="model",
    parts=[types.Part(text="Machine learning is...")]
)
```

## Sending Messages

With ADK, you send messages through the Runner:

```python
from google.adk.runners import Runner
from google.adk.sessions import InMemorySessionService

runner = Runner(
    agent=agent,
    app_name="my_app",
    session_service=InMemorySessionService(),
)

session = await runner.session_service.create_session(
    app_name="my_app",
    user_id="user-1",
)

# Send and get response
response = await runner.run_async(
    user_id="user-1",
    session_id=session.id,
    new_message=types.Content(
        role="user",
        parts=[types.Part(text="Hello!")]
    ),
)

# Read response
for event in response:
    if event.content and event.content.parts:
        print(event.content.parts[0].text)
```

## Multi-Part Messages

Like A2A, ADK supports multiple parts per message:

```python
# Text + data
message = types.Content(
    role="user",
    parts=[
        types.Part(text="Analyze this data:"),
        types.Part.from_data(
            data={"scores": [85, 92, 78]},
            mime_type="application/json"
        ),
    ]
)
```

## Under the Hood: A2A Translation

When deployed as an A2A server via `AdkApp`, ADK automatically translates between A2A JSON-RPC and internal types:

```
A2A JSON-RPC Request          ADK Internal
┌──────────────────┐         ┌──────────────────┐
│ message/send     │────────►│ Content(role,     │
│ params.message:  │         │   parts=[Part()])  │
│   role: "user"   │         └────────┬─────────┘
│   parts: [{text}]│                  │
└──────────────────┘                  ▼
                              Runner.run_async()
                                      │
A2A JSON-RPC Response                 ▼
┌──────────────────┐         ┌──────────────────┐
│ result:          │◄────────│ AgentResponse     │
│   artifacts: []  │         │   content.parts[] │
│   status: done   │         └──────────────────┘
└──────────────────┘
```

## Comparison: Sending a Message

**a2a-sdk** — Build JSON-RPC payload manually:
```python
payload = {
    "jsonrpc": "2.0",
    "id": str(uuid.uuid4()),
    "method": "message/send",
    "params": {
        "message": {
            "role": "user",
            "parts": [{"kind": "text", "text": "Hello"}],
            "messageId": str(uuid.uuid4()),
        }
    }
}
response = await client.post(url, json=payload)
```

**ADK** — Just pass content to the Runner:
```python
response = await runner.run_async(
    user_id="user-1",
    session_id=session.id,
    new_message=types.Content(
        role="user",
        parts=[types.Part(text="Hello")]
    ),
)
```
